meta.title: Team — Certified Environmental Experts | BTH Expert
meta.description: Meet the BTH Expert team. Engineers and experts accredited by the Algerian Ministry of Environment. Managing Director: Amine Lahmer. Based in Oran.

hero.heading: Experts & partners
hero.subheading: BTH Expert brings together international expertise forged within major Canadian engineering firms, combined with a deep grounding in the regulatory and industrial realities of Algeria.

members.name: Amine Lahmer
members.bio: An environmental engineer, Amine Lahmer gained several years of field experience within major Canadian engineering firms — notably GHD and AtkinsRéalis — on complex industrial projects in Algeria and Canada. Certified by the Algerian Petroleum Institute (Sonatrach/IAP), he has mastered Algerian regulatory frameworks and international standards in impact assessment, hazard studies, and industrial risk management. He co-founded BTH Expert with the ambition of bringing to Algerian industry a world-class expertise forged on the international stage.

members.name: Hakim Belgouini
members.bio: With over 40 years of experience in environmental studies and industrial projects in Algeria, Hakim Belgouini is a recognized authority in the sector across the west and centre of the country. As head of BTH Consult, he brings to each assignment a deep grounding in Algerian regulatory realities and a well-established network with wilaya administrations and industrial project owners.

partner.heading: BTH Consult Partnership
partner.description: BTH Expert and BTH Consult form a structured partnership for large-scale projects. This alliance complementarily unites Amine Lahmer's international expertise with Hakim Belgouini's 40 years of field practice in Algeria — ensuring complete technical and regulatory coverage on every assignment.

cta.heading: Let's work together
cta.description: Contact our team to discuss your environmental project.

meta.title: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in Algeria — BTH Expert
meta.description: Certified consultancy for conducting Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) in Algeria. Reports compliant with the requirements of the Ministry of Environment. Based in Oran.

hero.eyebrow: EIA · Accredited by the Ministry of Environment
hero.heading: Environmental Impact Assessment
hero.subheading: The EIA is the regulatory document that evaluates the impacts of a project on its natural, human, and socio-economic environment. Its completion is mandatory for all projects subject to environmental impact assessment under executive decree No. 07-145.

why.heading: Who is concerned?
why.description: Any industrial, mining, agricultural, or infrastructure project owner whose project appears on the list of works subject to EIA is legally required to produce this study before obtaining a construction or operating permit.

steps.title: Initial site assessment
steps.description: Inventory of environmental components: physical, biological, human, and landscape settings. Data collected on-site and from secondary sources.

steps.title: Impact analysis
steps.description: Identification, qualification, and prioritization of the direct and indirect impacts of the project on environmental components. Temporary and permanent impacts.

steps.title: Mitigation measures
steps.description: Definition of measures to suppress, reduce, and compensate for significant impacts. Each measure is costed and scheduled.

steps.title: Monitoring program
steps.description: Environmental monitoring indicators, control frequency, alert thresholds, and reporting procedures to the Ministry.

deliverable.heading: Deliverable
deliverable.description: A structured EIA report, compliant with the Algerian regulatory framework, ready for submission to the Wilaya Department of the Environment (DEW) and the Directorate of Industry and Mines (DIM).

faq.q: When is an Environmental Impact Assessment mandatory?
faq.a: As soon as your project appears on the regulatory list of works subject to environmental impact assessment (executive decree No. 07-145). The EIA is a prerequisite for obtaining a construction or operating permit.

faq.q: How long does an EIA take?
faq.a: Depending on the scale and sensitivity of the site, it can take from a few weeks to a few months. We define the timeline from the initial consultation.
The wilaya authorities — the Department of the Environment (DEW) and the Directorate of Industry and Mines (DIM).

cta.heading: Does your project require an EIA?
cta.description: Send us the technical details of your project. We assess the scope of the study and provide you with a quote within 48 hours.

meta.title: Hazard Study (EDD) for Classified Establishments — BTH Expert
meta.description: Certified consultancy for conducting Hazard Studies (EDD) in Algeria: major risk analysis, accident scenarios, effect zones and prevention plans. Based in Oran.

hero.heading: Hazard Study
hero.subheading: The Hazard Study identifies and characterizes the major accident risks of an industrial facility, evaluates their consequences, and defines control measures. It is required for classified establishments subject to authorization.

why.heading: Who is concerned?
why.description: Any operator of a classified installation for environmental protection subject to authorization — hydrocarbon storage, petrochemical units, hazardous material depots, pressure equipment — must produce a Hazard Study before commissioning and revise it after each substantial modification.

steps.title: Description & Environment
steps.description: Characterization of the installation, products, processes, and environmental vulnerability: populations, sensitive areas, and potential domino effects.

steps.title: Hazard Identification
steps.description: Systematic analysis of hazard potentials using recognized methods (APR, HAZOP), review of accident history, and identification of feared scenarios.

steps.title: Risk Analysis & Modelling
steps.description: Quantification of major scenarios: thermal, overpressure, and toxic effects, calculation of effect zones, and mapping of isolation distances.

steps.title: Risk Control
steps.description: Definition of prevention and protection barriers, the internal emergency plan, and response measures in the event of an accident.

deliverable.heading: Deliverable
deliverable.description: A Hazard Study report compliant with the Algerian regulatory framework, incorporating effect zone mapping and a risk control plan, ready for submission to the competent authorities.

faq.a: Any classified installation subject to authorization: hydrocarbon storage, petrochemical units, hazardous material depots, pressure equipment.

faq.q: What does the Hazard Study contain?
faq.a: Hazard identification, modelling of major scenarios (thermal, overpressure, and toxic effects), effect zone mapping, and a risk control plan.

faq.q: Does it need to be revised?
faq.a: Yes — after each substantial modification to the installation, its processes, or the products used.

cta.heading: Does your installation require an EDD?
cta.description: Send us the characteristics of your installation and processes. We will assess the scope of the study and provide you with a quote within 48 hours.

meta.title: HSE Audit & Regulatory Compliance — BTH Expert
meta.description: Hygiene, safety and environment (HSE) audit and regulatory compliance diagnostic for industrial sites in Algeria. Gap identification and prioritized action plan. Certified consultancy in Oran.

hero.heading: HSE Audit & Compliance
hero.subheading: The HSE Audit establishes the compliance status of a site with respect to hygiene, safety and environmental requirements. It highlights regulatory gaps and prioritizes the corrective actions to be taken.

why.heading: Who is concerned?
why.description: Any industrial or tertiary site wishing to secure its regulatory compliance, prepare for an inspection, initiate an ISO 14001 / 45001 approach, or manage its HSE risks ahead of an acquisition, expansion or certification.

steps.title: Document Review
steps.description: Analysis of existing authorizations, orders, registers and procedures against the regulatory obligations applicable to the site.

steps.title: Site Visit
steps.description: Inspection of facilities, workstations and safety devices; interviews with managers and observation of actual practices.

steps.title: Gap Analysis
steps.description: Comparison of the existing situation against legal and normative requirements, and assessment of the criticality of each identified gap.

steps.title: Prioritized Action Plan
steps.description: Costed and scheduled recommendations, ranked by risk level and regulatory deadline.

deliverable.heading: Deliverable
deliverable.description: A comprehensive HSE Audit report: regulatory compliance grid, gap register and prioritized action plan — a management tool for bringing the site into compliance.

faq.q: When should an HSE Audit be conducted?
faq.a: Before an inspection, ISO certification, acquisition or expansion — or simply to durably secure your compliance.
A regulatory compliance grid, a gap register and an action plan prioritized by risk level.
Yes — document review followed by a site visit, interviews and observation of actual practices.

cta.heading: Need an HSE Audit?
cta.description: Describe your site and your compliance objectives. We will define the audit scope and provide you with a proposal within 48 hours.

meta.title: Environmental Management Plan (PGE) — BTH Expert
meta.description: Development of Environmental Management Plans (PGE) for monitoring mitigation measures throughout the entire lifecycle of a project. Certified consultancy in Algeria, based in Oran.

hero.heading: Environmental Management Plan
hero.subheading: The Environmental Management Plan translates the commitments of the impact assessment into concrete measures: it organises the mitigation, monitoring and follow-up of a project's effects, from the construction phase through to operation.

why.heading: Who is concerned?
why.description: Any project developer whose impact assessment has prescribed mitigation and monitoring measures, as well as operators required to demonstrate the proper implementation of their environmental commitments to the authorities.

steps.title: Commitment scoping
steps.description: Review of the mitigation and compensation measures arising from the impact assessment and their translation into assigned and scheduled operational actions.

steps.title: Monitoring programme
steps.description: Definition of indicators, parameters to be measured, control frequencies and alert thresholds for each environmental component.

steps.title: Follow-up framework
steps.description: Organisation of responsibilities, reporting procedures and control arrangements during both the construction phase and the operational phase.

steps.title: Continuous improvement
steps.description: Review mechanisms, measure updates and non-conformity handling throughout the entire life of the project.

deliverable.heading: Deliverable
deliverable.description: An operational Environmental Management Plan: a measures matrix, a monitoring and follow-up programme, indicators and a reporting template, directly usable by project teams.

faq.q: What is a PGE used for?
faq.a: It translates the commitments of the impact assessment into concrete measures: mitigation, monitoring and follow-up throughout the entire lifecycle of the project.
Any project developer whose impact assessment has prescribed monitoring measures, and operators required to demonstrate their commitments.
A measures matrix, a monitoring programme, indicators and a reporting template ready to be used by your teams.

cta.heading: Structure your environmental monitoring?
cta.description: Share the prescriptions from your impact assessment with us. We will build a management plan tailored to your project and provide you with a quote within 48 hours.

list.title: Industry & Petrochemicals
list.tagline: Refining, cement plants, metallurgy, and surface treatments: processes with high impact and hazard potential.
list.intro: Industrial and petrochemical sites concentrate the most demanding environmental and safety challenges: atmospheric and aqueous discharges, hazardous product storage, and major accident risks. BTH Expert supports these facilities from impact assessment to risk management.

enjeux.description: Environmental Impact Assessments and Hazard Studies for establishments subject to authorization.

enjeux.description: Characterization and reduction of atmospheric and aqueous discharges and special waste.

enjeux.title: Major accident risk
enjeux.description: Scenarios, effect zones, and prevention plans for high-risk processes.

list.title: Energy & Hydrocarbons
list.tagline: Exploration, production, transport, and storage of hydrocarbons, and energy production: strategic and sensitive infrastructure.
list.intro: The energy and hydrocarbons sector operates extensive and sensitive infrastructure subject to a strict regulatory framework. BTH Expert intervenes across the entire chain, from production fields to transport and storage facilities.

enjeux.title: Installation safety

enjeux.title: Transition & efficiency
enjeux.description: Integration of energy challenges and renewable energy into projects.

list.title: Infrastructure & Construction

list.intro: Infrastructure and construction projects permanently transform the territory. BTH Expert supports project owners and contractors in anticipating impacts, securing authorizations, and organizing environmental monitoring of construction sites.

enjeux.title: Construction phase

enjeux.title: Authorizations

list.title: Agriculture & Hydraulics
list.tagline: Irrigated perimeters, dams, wastewater treatment plants, and agro-industrial projects: water resources at the heart of the challenges.
list.intro: Agriculture and hydraulics mobilize water resources and soils over large areas. BTH Expert assesses impacts, optimizes water management, and supports the compliance of agro-industrial and hydraulic projects.

enjeux.description: Preserving the quality and availability of water for ecosystems and human uses.

enjeux.title: Soils & environments

enjeux.title: Treatment & discharges

items.title: Environmental Impact Assessment of a Petrochemical Complex
items.excerpt: EIA and Hazard Study for a petrochemical unit in the Arzew industrial zone.
items.contexte: Installation of a new processing unit within an existing industrial zone, in proximity to third parties and sensitive environments. The project, subject to authorization, required an environmental assessment and characterization of major risks prior to review.
items.demarche: Initial site assessment, analysis of impacts on air, water and the human environment, modelling of major accident scenarios (thermal and overpressure effects) and definition of mitigation and risk control measures.
items.resultat: A complete regulatory file — EIA and EDD — submitted to the wilaya authorities, accompanied by an Environmental Management Plan and an internal emergency plan.

items.title: HSE Audit & Regulatory Compliance of a Cement Plant
items.excerpt: Regulatory compliance assessment and HSE action plan for an operational cement plant.
items.contexte: An operational cement plant sought to secure its regulatory compliance and structure its HSE approach ahead of an audit by the competent authorities.
items.demarche: Documentary review of authorizations and records, site visit, gap analysis against applicable requirements and prioritization of corrective actions.
items.resultat: A compliance matrix, a gap register and a prioritized action plan, serving as the basis for bringing the site into compliance over twelve months.

items.title: Environmental Impact Assessment of an Irrigated Perimeter
items.excerpt: Environmental assessment and water resource management for an agricultural irrigation project.
items.contexte: Development of an irrigated perimeter mobilizing water resources across a large agricultural area, with issues related to soils, natural environments and riparian uses.
items.demarche: Characterization of the initial state, assessment of effects on water resources and soils, and definition of an environmental monitoring and surveillance programme.
items.resultat: A validated Environmental Impact Assessment and an operational Environmental Management Plan for the construction and operational phases.

contact.heading: A project underway? Let's talk.
contact.description: Tell us about your project, your regulatory constraints, and your timeline. We will get back to you within 48 hours.

items.q: Which projects require an environmental impact assessment in Algeria?
items.a: Any project listed in executive decree 07-145 (classified installations, quarries, hydraulic projects, etc.) must undergo an EIA before any construction or operating permit is granted.

items.q: What is the average timeframe to obtain an environmental authorization?
items.a: Allow 2 to 6 months depending on the scale of the project and the wilaya. The study itself takes from a few weeks to several months; the public inquiry phase (for large projects) extends the timeline.

items.q: Does BTH Expert operate outside of Oran?
items.a: Yes. Based in Oran, we operate throughout Algeria, with a focus on western Algeria: Oran, Mostaganem, Tlemcen, Sidi Bel Abbès, Relizane, and neighboring wilayas.

items.q: What is a Ministry of Environment accreditation?
items.a: It is an official accreditation issued by the Ministry of Environment and Quality of Life, certifying that the certified consultancy is authorized to carry out EIAs and hazard studies. BTH Expert is accredited.

items.q: How much does an environmental study cost?
items.a: The cost depends on the type of study, the scale of the project, and its location. We provide a personalized quote following a free initial consultation. Contact us to discuss.

about.manifesto: No industrial project obtains its permit without a compliant environmental study. That is our profession, and our commitment.
about.body1: BTH Expert is an accredited environmental consulting and engineering firm, operating in the fields of environment, safety and hygiene across western Algeria.
about.body2: Impact assessments, HSE audits, environmental management plans — rigorous deliverables, ready for submission to the competent authorities.
